commands.review_summary:
The PR changes node-host exec fallback defaults and matching test helper defaults from `allowlist`/`on-miss` to `full`/`off`.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests 0. Total 0 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main and `v2026.6.6` pass undefined node-host defaults through helpers that resolve to `allowlist`/`on-miss`, while the canonical exec approval defaults and docs say `full`/`off`. I did not reproduce the full WSL2/Windows Companion App setup.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed to choose among the linked fix candidates and confirm real behavior proof for the reported environment before merge.

target.title: fix(exec): align node-host exec security/ask defaults with exec-approvals constants
target.body:
## Summary
- resolveExecSecurity and resolveExecAsk in node-host/invoke.ts fell back to "allowlist" and "on-miss" when no explicit value was provided
- These defaults conflicted with DEFAULT_SECURITY="full" and DEFAULT_ASK="off" in exec-approvals.ts and exec-defaults.ts
- When exec-approvals.json configured security=full and ask=off, the merge via minSecurity/maxAsk lowered security to "allowlist" and raised ask to "on-miss", causing exec host=node commands to be rejected with "No matching rule; default policy applied"

## Verification
- Ran pnpm exec vitest run src/node-host/invoke-system-run.test.ts src/node-host/exec-policy.test.ts — 47 tests passed
- Updated mirror functions in test file to match production defaults

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** node-host exec security/ask defaults now match the DEFAULT_SECURITY="full" and DEFAULT_ASK="off" constants in exec-approvals.ts, preventing unintended policy downgrade when exec-approvals.json is configured

**Environment tested:** Node 24.x on Linux, pnpm vitest

**Steps run after the patch:** Ran targeted unit tests for invoke-system-run and exec-policy

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668001344/openclaw

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  47 passed (47)
   Start at  18:33:54
   Duration  1.56s (transform 855ms, setup 0ms, import 1.06s, tests 259ms, environment 0ms)
```

**Observed result after the fix:** All exec-policy tests pass with aligned defaults. Commands with security=full, ask=off in exec-approvals.json will now correctly evaluate to full/off instead of allowlist/on-miss.

**Not tested:** Live Companion App exec on WSL2/Windows with a real exec-approvals.json configuration

## Root Cause
- Root cause: node-host/invoke.ts resolveExecSecurity and resolveExecAsk used hardcoded fallback values ("allowlist" / "on-miss") that contradicted the canonical DEFAULT_SECURITY="full" and DEFAULT_ASK="off" in infra/exec-approvals.ts and agents/exec-defaults.ts
- Missing detection/guardrail: no consistency check between node-host defaults and exec-approvals constants; the merge via minSecurity/maxAsk silently downgraded user-configured policy

Closes #93518
target.author: zoowh
